Area: === SITE SHELL — EDITOR LOADS IN SITE-AWARE MODE ===

Steps to Perform: 1. Open /projects/{id}/editor for any project
What Should Happen: Three-panel layout loads: left rail (pages + site sections), center canvas (current page), right panel (inspector). No errors.

Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor
What Should Happen: Left rail has a 'Pages' section listing all pages in the site with their titles

Test Case: Left rail shows Site section below pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor
What Should Happen: Below the pages list, the left rail shows site-level items: Settings, Theme, Assets, Actions

Test Case: Current page is highlighted in page list
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor
What Should Happen: The currently displayed page (homepage by default) is highlighted/selected in the page list

Test Case: Top bar shows site and page context
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor
What Should Happen: Top bar shows the site name and the current page name so the user always knows where they are

Test Case: Right panel changes based on selection
Steps to Perform: 1. Click a page in the left rail 2. Click a section on the canvas 3. Click the header area
What Should Happen: Right panel updates to show: page settings, section inspector, or header settings respectively depending on what is selected

Test Case: Click a different page to switch
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor (starts on homepage) 2. Click 'About' page in the left rail
What Should Happen: Canvas updates to show the About page sections. Page list highlights 'About' as active.

Steps to Perform: 1. Switch from Homepage to Services page
What Should Happen: All sections on the canvas change to show the Services page content. Shared header and footer remain.

Test Case: Sections list updates on page switch
Steps to Perform: 1. Switch pages
What Should Happen: The sections list (if visible) updates to show sections belonging to the newly selected page, not the old page

Steps to Perform: 1. Make an unsaved change on the current page 2. Click a different page in the left rail
What Should Happen: A prompt appears asking to save or discard changes before switching

Steps to Perform: 1. Make a change 2. Switch pages and choose 'Save'
What Should Happen: Changes are saved. Switching back to the original page shows the saved changes.

Steps to Perform: 1. Make a change 2. Switch pages and choose 'Discard'
What Should Happen: Changes are reverted. Switching back shows the original content.

Steps to Perform: 1. Look at the Pages section in the left rail
What Should Happen: A '+' or 'Add Page' button is visible below the page list

Test Case: Click add shows page type picker
Steps to Perform: 1. Click '+ Add Page'
What Should Happen: A menu or dialog appears with page type options: About, Services, Contact, Pricing, FAQ, Custom

Steps to Perform: 1. Click '+' 2. Select 'About' 3. Enter title 'About Us' 4. Confirm
What Should Happen: New page 'About Us' appears in the page list. Canvas switches to the new (empty) page.

Test Case: New page has a default slug
Steps to Perform: 1. Create a new page titled 'Our Services'
What Should Happen: The page automatically gets a slug like 'our-services'

Test Case: New page appears in navigation (if showInNav is true)
Steps to Perform: 1. Create a new page
What Should Happen: The shared header navigation updates to include the new page link

Steps to Perform: 1. Right-click or use the three-dot menu on a page 2. Click 'Rename' 3. Type 'About Our Team' 4. Confirm
What Should Happen: Page title updates in the page list and in the header navigation

Steps to Perform: 1. Rename a page
What Should Happen: If the slug was auto-generated, it may update to match. If manually set, it stays as-is.

Steps to Perform: 1. Use the three-dot menu on a page 2. Click 'Duplicate'
What Should Happen: A copy of the page appears in the list with a title like 'About Us (Copy)'. All sections are copied.

Steps to Perform: 1. Duplicate a page 2. Open both pages
What Should Happen: Each page has independent content. Editing one does not affect the other.

Steps to Perform: 1. Duplicate a page
What Should Happen: The copy gets a different slug (e.g., 'about-us-copy' instead of 'about-us')

Steps to Perform: 1. Use the three-dot menu on a non-homepage page 2. Click 'Delete' 3. Confirm
What Should Happen: Page is removed from the list, canvas, and navigation

Steps to Perform: 1. Click 'Delete' on a page
What Should Happen: A confirmation dialog appears asking 'Are you sure?' before deleting

Steps to Perform: 1. Try to delete the homepage
What Should Happen: Delete is prevented. Either the option is disabled or an error message explains that the homepage cannot be deleted.

Steps to Perform: 1. Delete all pages except one 2. Try to delete the last remaining page
What Should Happen: Deletion is prevented. A site must have at least one page.

Steps to Perform: 1. Delete a page that was showing in nav
What Should Happen: The page link disappears from the header navigation

Steps to Perform: 1. Use up arrow or drag a page above another in the list
What Should Happen: The page moves up in the list. Navigation order updates to match.

Steps to Perform: 1. Use down arrow or drag a page below another in the list
What Should Happen: The page moves down in the list. Navigation order updates.

Test Case: Page order persists after save and refresh
Steps to Perform: 1. Reorder pages 2. Save 3. Refresh browser
What Should Happen: Pages appear in the new order

Test Case: Set a different page as homepage
Steps to Perform: 1. Use the three-dot menu on a non-homepage page 2. Click 'Set as Homepage'
What Should Happen: The selected page gets a homepage badge. The previous homepage loses its badge.

Steps to Perform: 1. Look at the page list
What Should Happen: The homepage has a visible indicator (badge, icon, or label) marking it as the homepage

Steps to Perform: 1. Set 'Services' as homepage 2. Visit /p/{project-slug}
What Should Happen: The Services page content is served (not the old homepage)

Steps to Perform: 1. Use the three-dot menu on a page 2. Toggle 'Show in Navigation' off
What Should Happen: The page disappears from the header navigation links but remains in the page list

Test Case: Hidden page still accessible by URL
Steps to Perform: 1. Hide a page from nav 2. Visit /p/{project-slug}/{page-slug}
What Should Happen: The page still renders correctly when accessed directly by URL

Steps to Perform: 1. Toggle navigation visibility back on for a hidden page
What Should Happen: The page reappears in the header navigation

Test Case: Header visible on canvas above page sections
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor on any page
What Should Happen: A shared header area appears at the top of the canvas showing logo/site name and navigation links

Test Case: Header is the same on every page
Steps to Perform: 1. Switch between multiple pages
What Should Happen: The same header (logo, nav, CTA) appears at the top of every page

Steps to Perform: 1. Click on the header area on the canvas
What Should Happen: The header gets a selection indicator. The right panel switches to header settings.

Steps to Perform: 1. Select the header 2. Change variant in the right panel (e.g., 'simple' to 'with-cta')
What Should Happen: The header layout changes on the canvas

Test Case: Edit navigation items in header inspector
Steps to Perform: 1. Select header 2. Look at the navigation items in the inspector
What Should Happen: Shows the list of pages in nav order. Can reorder, hide, or show individual items.

Steps to Perform: 1. Select header 2. Find the CTA button settings
What Should Happen: Can edit the CTA button text and link it to an action (e.g., 'Contact Us' → scroll to contact)

Test Case: Header shows on all preview modes
Steps to Perform: 1. Switch between Desktop, Tablet, Mobile preview
What Should Happen: Header is visible and adapts layout for each device size

Test Case: Footer visible on canvas below page sections
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor on any page
What Should Happen: A shared footer area appears at the bottom of the canvas

Test Case: Footer is the same on every page
Steps to Perform: 1. Switch between multiple pages
What Should Happen: The same footer appears at the bottom of every page

Steps to Perform: 1. Click on the footer area on the canvas
What Should Happen: The footer gets a selection indicator. The right panel switches to footer settings.

Steps to Perform: 1. Select footer 2. Change variant (simple / multi-column / legal-heavy)
What Should Happen: Footer layout changes on the canvas

Steps to Perform: 1. Select footer 2. Edit column titles and links in the inspector
What Should Happen: Footer content updates on the canvas

Steps to Perform: 1. Select footer 2. Edit the copyright text
What Should Happen: Footer copyright text updates

Steps to Perform: 1. Edit footer 2. Switch pages
What Should Happen: Footer edits are visible on every page

Steps to Perform: 1. Look at the header navigation on the canvas
What Should Happen: Navigation links correspond to pages that have 'Show in Nav' enabled, in the correct order

Test Case: Adding a page adds a nav link
Steps to Perform: 1. Create a new page with 'Show in Nav' on
What Should Happen: A new navigation link appears in the header

Steps to Perform: 1. Reorder pages in the left rail
What Should Happen: Navigation link order updates to match

Test Case: Nav links are clickable in preview mode
Steps to Perform: 1. Enter Preview mode 2. Click a navigation link
What Should Happen: The canvas switches to show the clicked page

Steps to Perform: 1. Click different pages in the left rail
What Should Happen: Canvas shows only the sections belonging to the selected page (not other pages' sections)

Steps to Perform: 1. Switch to 'Services' page 2. Add a new section
What Should Happen: Section is added to the Services page, not the homepage

Steps to Perform: 1. Duplicate, move, delete a section on a specific page 2. Switch to another page
What Should Happen: The changes only affected the page they were made on

Steps to Perform: 1. Select a page 2. Open page settings in the right panel 3. Edit the SEO title
What Should Happen: Title field updates. Saved to that specific page.

Steps to Perform: 1. Edit the meta description for a page
What Should Happen: Description field updates and saves per-page

Steps to Perform: 1. Edit the slug for a page (e.g., change 'about' to 'about-us')
What Should Happen: Slug updates. Published URL changes accordingly.

Steps to Perform: 1. Try to set a page slug to the same value as another page's slug
What Should Happen: Error message: slug must be unique within the site

Steps to Perform: 1. Set one page to 'Published' and another to 'Draft'
What Should Happen: Only the published page is accessible to visitors. Draft page shows only to the owner.

Test Case: Add section to a specific page
Steps to Perform: 1. Switch to a page 2. Click '+' to add a section 3. Choose type and variant
What Should Happen: Section is added to that page only. Other pages are unaffected.

Steps to Perform: 1. Move a section up or down on the current page
What Should Happen: Section order changes on that page only

Steps to Perform: 1. Duplicate a section
What Should Happen: A copy appears on the same page

Steps to Perform: 1. Toggle section visibility
What Should Happen: Section visibility changes on that page only

Steps to Perform: 1. Select a section 2. Change its variant in the inspector
What Should Happen: Section layout/style changes on the canvas

Test Case: Inline text editing works per page
Steps to Perform: 1. Click on a heading on a specific page 2. Edit text
What Should Happen: Text changes on that page. Other pages' headings are unaffected.

Test Case: Click on canvas background selects nothing
Steps to Perform: 1. Click empty space on the canvas
What Should Happen: Right panel shows site overview or default state. No section or page is selected.

Test Case: Click a page in left rail shows page settings
Steps to Perform: 1. Click a page name in the page list
What Should Happen: Right panel switches to page settings (title, slug, SEO, type, nav visibility, status)

Test Case: Click a section shows section inspector
Steps to Perform: 1. Click a section on the canvas
What Should Happen: Right panel shows section inspector with variant, style, content fields

Test Case: Click the header shows header inspector
Steps to Perform: 1. Click the header area on the canvas
What Should Happen: Right panel shows header settings (variant, sticky, logo, nav items, CTA)

Test Case: Click the footer shows footer inspector
Steps to Perform: 1. Click the footer area on the canvas
What Should Happen: Right panel shows footer settings (variant, columns, links, copyright)

Test Case: Clicking Site Settings in left rail shows site inspector
Steps to Perform: 1. Click 'Settings' under the Site section in the left rail
What Should Happen: Right panel shows site-level settings (site name, contact info, social links)

Test Case: Clicking Theme in left rail shows theme panel
Steps to Perform: 1. Click 'Theme' under Site in left rail
What Should Happen: Right panel shows brand/theme settings (colors, fonts, tone) — these are site-wide

Test Case: Preview mode shows full page with header and footer
Steps to Perform: 1. Click 'Preview'
What Should Happen: Left and right panels hide. Canvas shows the full page including shared header and footer.

Steps to Perform: 1. In Preview mode, click Desktop icon
What Should Happen: Canvas takes full width

Steps to Perform: 1. Click Tablet icon
What Should Happen: Canvas shrinks to tablet width (~768px)

Steps to Perform: 1. Click Mobile icon
What Should Happen: Canvas shrinks to mobile width (~375px)

Steps to Perform: 1. Switch to Services page 2. Enter Preview mode
What Should Happen: Services page content is shown with header and footer

Steps to Perform: 1. Enter Preview mode 2. Click a nav link or switch pages
What Should Happen: Canvas updates to show the new page in preview mode

Test Case: Old single-page project opens without errors
Steps to Perform: 1. Open an existing project that was created before multi-page support
What Should Happen: Editor loads without errors. The existing page appears as the homepage.

Steps to Perform: 1. Open an old project in the new editor
What Should Happen: The existing page is treated as the homepage. It appears first in the page list with a homepage badge.

Steps to Perform: 1. Visit /p/{slug} for an existing published project
What Should Happen: Page renders correctly with the same content as before

Test Case: Legacy footer section migrated to site footer
Steps to Perform: 1. Open an old project with a footer section
What Should Happen: The footer content appears in the shared footer area (site-level), not duplicated

Steps to Perform: 1. Call GET /api/projects/{id}/page
What Should Happen: Returns the homepage data (backward compatible)

Steps to Perform: 1. Edit a section on a page 2. Click Save
What Should Happen: Changes are saved. 'Unsaved' badge disappears.

Steps to Perform: 1. Save changes 2. Refresh browser
What Should Happen: All changes are still present on the correct page

Steps to Perform: 1. Edit the header or footer 2. Click Save
What Should Happen: Site-level changes are persisted

Steps to Perform: 1. Save site settings changes 2. Refresh
What Should Happen: Header, footer, navigation changes are preserved

Steps to Perform: 1. Create a new page 2. Refresh browser
What Should Happen: The new page still appears in the page list

Steps to Perform: 1. Reorder pages 2. Save 3. Refresh
What Should Happen: Pages remain in the new order

Steps to Perform: 1. Add/remove pages from nav 2. Save 3. Refresh
What Should Happen: Navigation reflects the saved state

Steps to Perform: 1. Open a project with only the homepage
What Should Happen: Editor works normally. Page list shows one page. No errors.

Steps to Perform: 1. Create 10+ pages
What Should Happen: Page list is scrollable. Performance is acceptable.

Steps to Perform: 1. Create a page with a very long title (100+ characters)
What Should Happen: Title truncates in the page list. Full title shown on hover or in page settings.

Steps to Perform: 1. Create two pages 2. Try to give them the same slug
What Should Happen: Error message prevents saving duplicate slugs within the same project

Steps to Perform: 1. Try to delete the homepage
What Should Happen: Action is blocked with an explanation. User must set another page as homepage first.

Steps to Perform: 1. Create a page with title: About & Contact — Us!
What Should Happen: Title displays correctly. Slug is auto-sanitized (e.g., 'about-contact-us').

Steps to Perform: 1. Click through multiple pages quickly
What Should Happen: Canvas updates correctly without errors or stale content showing

Steps to Perform: 1. Publish the site 2. Visit /p/{project-slug}
What Should Happen: Homepage renders with header, page content, and footer

Steps to Perform: 1. Create and publish an About page 2. Visit /p/{project-slug}/about
What Should Happen: About page renders with shared header, About content, and shared footer

Test Case: Navigation links work on published site
Steps to Perform: 1. Visit published site 2. Click navigation links
What Should Happen: Each link navigates to the correct page

Test Case: Draft pages not visible to visitors
Steps to Perform: 1. Set a page to 'Draft' status 2. Try to visit its URL as a non-owner
What Should Happen: Page returns 404 or a 'not available' message

Steps to Perform: 1. Set a page to 'Published' 2. Visit its URL as a non-owner
What Should Happen: Page renders correctly
